coverage_message: One or more required capabilities lack sufficient source coverage.

sources.name: Global Disaster Alert and Coordination System
sources.owner: European Commission Joint Research Centre and United Nations
known_limitations: Hazard alerts do not by themselves establish operational logistics disruption.
Publication timing and alert levels vary by hazard type.
No official fixed polling cadence or public rate-limit quota was found; expected_cadence_minutes is unknown, not a proven six-minute cadence.

Because expected_cadence_minutes is unknown, source-health evaluation currently derives its fresh/stale boundary from half of max_stale_minutes; this derived boundary is an implementation default, not an official GDACS cadence, and any future production-enablement change must define this explicitly rather than treating the derived value as fact.
Responses are limited to 100 records per page; callers must page with pagenumber (and optionally pagesize) to retrieve a full result set.
GDACS alert levels and impact estimates are model outputs and must remain a source signal (source_alert_level), never platform logistics severity.
Automatic notifications may contain uncertainty or error, do not replace national authorities, and have no guaranteed completeness or timeliness.

sources.name: Thai Meteorological Department warnings and CAP services
known_limitations: Machine-readable endpoint and update cadence must pass an integration test before enablement.
A warning indicates hazard potential, not confirmed transport or facility disruption.

TMD's copyright notice permits non-commercial public republication with source attribution, but its separate website policy states that deep-linking to internal pages requires written permission; these statements are not fully aligned, so licence_status remains pending_review and the conflict is not assumed resolved.

The controlled manual workflow has validated content type and parseability of the live English and Thai endpoints (see the WO-003 evidence below): both currently serve a well-formed RSS envelope, not a direct CAP 1.2 alert. machine_readable_status therefore remains unverified specifically for direct CAP 1.2 compatibility at these URLs -- not because validation is pending, but because the validated result does not establish it -- and stays unverified until either the served content changes or a separate reviewed decision treats the RSS envelope itself as the machine-readable contract.

WO-003 controlled live-test evidence (2026-07-23, both the English and Thai URLs): HTTP 200, Content-Type text/xml, no redirect, no ETag or Last-Modified, XML root local name rss -- the strict CAP 1.2 parser correctly rejected both with 'expected {urn:oasis:names:tc:emergency:cap:1.2}alert, received rss' and zero staging records were emitted.

This conflicts with two official descriptions that remain unresolved by inference: the WMO Register of Alerting Authorities still lists both TMD URLs as CAP Feed URLs (https://alertingauthority.wmo.int/authorities.php?recId=164), and TMD's own RSS service page presents "CAP Warning" under "RSS format open data" (https://www.tmd.go.th/en/service/rss).

One observation date does not establish cadence or permanence; endpoint behavior may change, and this contract does not assume the RSS envelope is a permanent replacement for a direct CAP 1.2 alert at these URLs.

A controlled, discovery-only RSS-envelope path exists (collectors/adapters/rss_discovery.py, manual workflow tmd_operation=rss_discovery) to classify and inspect the envelope without weakening the strict CAP parser; it retains only bounded structural metadata, never fetches a discovered item link or enclosure, and never produces a staging record.

WO-005 (2026-07-23) used the RSS discovery path above, run under human-triggered gates, to observe candidate CAP filenames referenced by both the English and Thai RSS feeds; run IDs, artifact IDs, and response hashes are recorded in docs/tmd_candidate_cap_validation.md. This is discovery-only evidence -- candidate reachability, CAP-1.2-ness, cross-language semantic equivalence, and publication suitability were not established by WO-005.

WO-006 (Implementation v0.2.2) added a controlled, manually triggered, single-candidate CAP validation path (collectors/adapters/tmd_candidate.py, TmdCapAdapter.validate_candidate, manual workflow tmd_operation=candidate_cap_validation) that accepts only a bounded candidate reference -- never an arbitrary URL -- derives the fetch target from fixed host/port/path-prefix/filename-grammar policy, resolves and pins DNS with fail-closed non-global-address rejection, makes exactly one physical HTTPS GET, and runs the unchanged strict CAP 1.2 parser. See docs/tmd_candidate_cap_validation.md for the full security boundary, retained/excluded fields, and evidence. WO-006 is implementation only: no live candidate fetch was performed under it, and a separate WO-007 plus human approval is required before any live candidate fetch, source enablement, or publication.

sources.name: New York Fed Global Supply Chain Pressure Index
sources.owner: Federal Reserve Bank of New York
blockers: No controlled live validation has been performed; the endpoint field is still null and no published download URL has been confirmed by observation.
Reuse and redistribution terms have not been reviewed against the publisher's stated conditions, so reuse_status and redistribution_status remain unknown.
known_limitations: Monthly baseline indicator; not suitable for real-time event detection.
Download endpoint must be verified before enablement.

WO-010: no controlled live validation could be performed, so the published file location, format and cadence remain unverified. The fixture-first adapter (collectors/adapters/csv_series.py, parser gscpi_v1) parses the documented CSV shape only.
The index is a global measure. It cannot establish a Thailand-specific or lane-specific conclusion on its own.

sources.name: Thai Customs import and export statistics
blockers: No controlled live validation has been performed; no stable machine-readable download URL has been confirmed by observation, and the endpoint field remains null.
Reuse and redistribution terms have not been reviewed, so reuse_status and redistribution_status remain unknown.
The statistics portal is an interactive report builder; whether it exposes a stable CSV export without session state is unresolved.
known_limitations: The website can revise published statistics.
Export workflow and stable download URL require integration testing.

WO-010: no controlled live validation could be performed. The trade series used by the Ocean MVP are therefore synthetic test fixtures, labelled synthetic_test_fixture in every record, and no published Thailand trade value is claimed.
Published customs statistics are all-mode totals unless a mode dimension is explicitly provided; attributing them to ocean freight without that dimension would be an invented precision.

sources.name: EPPO retail fuel price publications
sources.owner: Energy Policy and Planning Office, Thailand
blockers: No controlled live validation has been performed; the endpoint field is null and the page is client-rendered, so a machine-readable extraction path is unconfirmed.
Reuse and redistribution terms have not been reviewed, so reuse_status and redistribution_status remain unknown.
known_limitations: Dynamic page rendering may expose placeholders before the final data loads.
Parsed values require date, unit, and effective-date validation before publication.
WO-010: no controlled live validation could be performed. Fuel series used by the Ocean MVP are synthetic test fixtures, labelled synthetic_test_fixture in every record.
Retail pump prices are a domestic cost-context indicator. They are not bunker fuel prices and must not be presented as a shipping fuel cost.

sources.name: Bank of Thailand foreign exchange rates
blockers: Requires an API key. No credential-handling mechanism exists in this repository and WO-010 does not introduce one.
No controlled live validation has been performed.
Reuse and redistribution terms have not been reviewed.
known_limitations: The developer portal requires account registration and an API key. A key is a credential and must never be committed to this repository, so enablement additionally requires a secrets-handling decision that WO-010 does not make.
A reference exchange rate is a cost-context indicator. A change in it does not by itself establish a change in any freight, bunker or landed cost actually paid.
WO-010: no controlled live validation could be performed; the endpoint field is null and the response envelope is unverified.

sources.name: World Bank Commodity Price Data
blockers: No XLSX parser exists in this repository; adding one is outside the WO-010 scope and no fixture test has been written for this source.
No controlled live validation has been performed.
Reuse and redistribution terms have not been reviewed against the publisher's stated conditions.
known_limitations: Global commodity benchmarks are energy-cost context. They are not a Thailand fuel price and not a bunker price paid by any carrier.
The published workbook format is XLSX; no XLSX parser is implemented by WO-010, so the fixture-first adapter covers the CSV shape only.
WO-010: no controlled live validation could be performed; the endpoint field is null.

sources.name: IMF PortWatch port activity and trade estimates
blockers: No controlled live validation has been performed; no ArcGIS feature-service query URL has been confirmed by observation and the endpoint field remains null.
Reuse and redistribution terms have not been reviewed.
Whether derived daily estimates may be republished, as opposed to linked, is unresolved.
known_limitations: PortWatch figures are model-derived estimates from vessel tracking, not port-authority reported throughput. They must be labelled as estimates and must never be presented as official port statistics.
Port call counts measure how many vessels called. They are a volume metric and can never on their own establish congestion, waiting time or berth delay.
WO-010: no controlled live validation could be performed; the ArcGIS query endpoint, its envelope shape and its rate limits are all unverified.

sources.name: UNCTAD maritime transport and connectivity indicators
sources.owner: United Nations Conference on Trade and Development
blockers: No controlled live validation has been performed; no stable machine-readable download URL has been confirmed by observation.
Reuse and redistribution terms have not been reviewed.
known_limitations: Connectivity indices are structural measures published quarterly or annually. They describe how well connected an economy's liner network is; they are not an operational condition and cannot detect an event.
WO-010: no controlled live validation could be performed; the endpoint field is null.

sources.name: Port Authority of Thailand public notices
blockers: No machine-readable notice feed has been confirmed; the endpoint field is null.
Publication cadence is unknown, so no automated schedule can be justified.
Reuse and redistribution terms have not been reviewed.
No controlled live validation has been performed.
known_limitations: An official notice is evidence of the notice itself. It is not automatically evidence of an operational impact on any particular lane, service or organization.
No machine-readable notice feed has been confirmed. Whether the site publishes an RSS or Atom feed is unverified.
Publication cadence is unknown, so no automated collection schedule is justified and none is configured.
WO-010: no controlled live validation could be performed.

sources.name: Panama Canal Authority advisories to shipping
blockers: No machine-readable advisory feed has been confirmed; the endpoint field is null.
Advisory bodies are commonly PDF; no PDF parsing path exists.
Reuse and redistribution terms have not been reviewed.
No controlled live validation has been performed.
known_limitations: A canal advisory establishes the restriction it states. It does not establish which Thailand services actually route through the canal, and it therefore cannot on its own establish a Thailand lane impact.
Advisories are frequently published as PDF attachments; no PDF parser exists in this repository and none is added by WO-010.
WO-010: no controlled live validation could be performed.

sources.name: Suez Canal Authority circulars
blockers: No machine-readable circular feed has been confirmed; the endpoint field is null.
Circular bodies are commonly PDF; no PDF parsing path exists.
Reuse and redistribution terms have not been reviewed.
No controlled live validation has been performed.
known_limitations: A circular establishes the rule or toll it states. Whether a given Thailand service transits the canal, and whether it was affected, requires separate evidence.
Circulars are frequently published as PDF attachments; no PDF parser exists in this repository.
WO-010: no controlled live validation could be performed.

sources.name: Maritime and Port Authority of Singapore port marine notices
sources.owner: Maritime and Port Authority of Singapore
blockers: No machine-readable notice feed has been confirmed; the endpoint field is null.
Reuse and redistribution terms have not been reviewed.
No controlled live validation has been performed.
known_limitations: Singapore is a transshipment hub for Thailand cargo, so a notice here is relevant to Thailand only through the transshipment mechanism, which must be stated rather than assumed.
A port marine notice is evidence of the notice, not of an operational effect on any particular service.
WO-010: no controlled live validation could be performed.

sources.name: Human-reviewed official operational notice intake
blockers: No notice has yet been recorded through this path, so the intake exists but currently contributes no coverage.
known_limitations: This is a bounded manual intake path, not an automated collector. It makes no network request of any kind.

A human records a bounded reference to an official notice they have read at the publisher's own site, together with the publisher, the notice reference, the publication date and a claim of at most 600 characters. Full notice text is never copied into this repository.
Coverage is whatever a human happened to record. Absence of a notice here is never evidence that no notice was published.
Every record must still name the underlying publisher as the evidence source; this contract is the intake mechanism, not the authority.

sources.name: Free structured public news discovery
blockers: No controlled live validation has been performed; the query endpoint and its rate limits are unverified and the endpoint field is null.
Reuse terms have not been reviewed. Only link-level redistribution would be considered even if they were.
known_limitations: This is a DISCOVERY source only. Every record it produces carries evidence_role 'discovery_only' and can never be the sole evidence for a material impact conclusion.
Only headline-level metadata and the publisher's own URL are retained. Article bodies are never fetched, stored or republished.
Coverage is skewed toward English-language and heavily syndicated outlets, so absence of a lead is not evidence that nothing happened.
Automated relevance matching produces false positives; every lead requires primary-source verification before it can support any conclusion.
WO-010: no controlled live validation could be performed; the endpoint field is null.

event_impact_separation_examples: EVT-20231030-001: event severity moderate vs worst impact severity low
EVT-20231218-001: event severity high vs worst impact severity moderate
EVT-20240326-001: event severity high vs worst impact severity none
EVT-20240614-002: event severity low vs worst impact severity none
